summary: Frontend-only verification of SCP Site Overseer reskin on https://scp-terminal-3.preview.emergentagent.com at 430x932 mobile viewport. All 30 critical checks passed. App now boots instantly to TERMINAL/Home (no booting/uplink block) from bundled config + default state. The new dark sci-fi control-room theme is in place: card layout, red rule under header, classification badges, solid-yellow primary buttons, and the 5-icon bottom tab bar (TERMINAL/ARCHIVE/ENCOUNTERS/ARMORY/DOSSIER) with red active color. Tab navigation, direct URL routing, section pills (Store/Research/Loadout and Operations/Breach and Personnel/Awards/System), purchases, operations dispatch, full multi-phase breach flow (select->assess->deploy->scenario->minigame->result), custom-SCP filing + expand toggle/delete, canon catalog, dossier expansion, achievements pill and NewGame difficulty/iron-man controls all work. CC BY-SA 3.0 attribution is visible on the TERMINAL footer.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js linking.prefixes is an empty array; on web React Navigation derives the origin from window.location, so this works for the preview host but is brittle if the app is ever served from a sub-path. Consider passing the actual origin so deep-links remain stable.

ui.js: `tab-<key>` testIDs are emitted by every mounted Screen, and native-stack keeps prior screens mounted on web, so the DOM contains multiple elements with the same testid (one per stack entry). Tests must use locator.last (top of stack). Consider gating TabBar rendering to the focused screen via useIsFocused() or scoping testids per screen to avoid this duplication.
Breach.js MiniGame setInterval at 28ms is fine functionally but quite CPU-hungry on web; consider requestAnimationFrame or 60ms tick for battery.
Archives.js: CLASSES list is hardcoded; if bootstrapData ever adds Neutralised/Explained classes, frontend will need a code change. Sourcing from boot.scp_classes would future-proof it.
store.js: GameProvider initialises from bundled bootstrapData and DEFAULT_STATE so the dashboard renders even when the backend body stalls — this is the right resilience pattern for this environment.
All interactive elements expose stable data-testid values, which made automation deterministic.
test_credentials: None - anonymous device id, no auth.
seed_data_creation: Filed 1 custom SCP ('Test Anomaly QA', class Keter, breachable) during the run. Bought 1 flashlight (CR 500->450). Dispatched op SCP_999 (CR 450->600, RC 200->280). Completed 1 breach attempt against breach-target-0. NewGame difficulty set to Hard + Iron-Man toggled ON but CONFIRM was deliberately not clicked, so device state remains: difficulty Hard from previous run, ~650 CR / 300 RC, 1 custom SCP. Next agent can reset via the System pill if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use viewport 430x932 and the live URL https://scp-terminal-3.preview.emergentagent.com. IMPORTANT QUIRK: native-stack on web keeps previous screens mounted, so every common testid (tab-*, section-*, deploy-btn, lock-btn, archive-*, etc.) typically has multiple matches in the DOM. Always use page.locator('[data-testid="..."]').last to click the currently visible one, or use page.evaluate() with getBoundingClientRect() to find the visible candidate. Direct URL deep-links work (/store, /research, /loadout, /operations, /breach, /archives, /personnel, /achievements, /new-game) and are the most reliable way to reach a specific screen after a reload, because '/' resets to Home. Helper function to detect the visible screen is to iterate the known *-screen testids and check isVisible(). The breach mini-game needs exactly 3 lock-btn taps (with ~400ms between) followed by ~1.5s wait for the result panel to mount (breach-again testid).
rca of the issue: No bugs found in this iteration. Previous iteration_5 already verified the booting-screen fix; this iteration confirms the reskin + nav restructure + 5-tab bottom bar work end-to-end.
